title: SageAttention: Accurate 8-Bit Attention for Plug-and-play Inference Acceleration
core_contribution: SageAttention proposes a plug-and-play INT8 quantization method for attention that achieves 2.1x speedup over FlashAttention2 with negligible accuracy loss, by combining K-smoothing, mixed INT8/FP16 precision, and adaptive per-layer kernel selection.
summary: SageAttention addresses attention quantization challenges with three key techniques: (1) smoothing K by subtracting channel-wise mean exploiting softmax shift-invariance; (2) INT8 per-block quantization for QK^T with FP16 for PV; (3) adaptive quantization selecting fastest kernel per layer. Achieves 341 TOPS on RTX4090, 2.1x faster than FlashAttention2 with negligible end-to-end metrics loss across 6 diverse models.
key_findings: K matrix channel-wise outliers eliminated via mean subtraction (softmax shift-invariance), improving worst-case CosSim from 30.6% to 99.3%
INT8 quantization more accurate than FP8 (INT8: 99.54% vs E4M3: 92.83%) and 2x faster on RTX4090
FP16 accumulator for PV matmul achieves identical accuracy to FP32 while doubling speed
Achieves 341 TOPS on RTX4090, 2.1x faster than FlashAttention2
limitations: Only evaluated on RTX4090/RTX3090; no datacenter GPU results
Only targets inference forward pass; backward pass not addressed
Adaptive quantization requires offline calibration per model

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables post-training quantized attention as generic acceleration orthogonal to weight quantization
infra_impact.kernel: INT8 attention kernel template in Triton achieving 52% of theoretical peak
infra_impact.framework: Plug-and-play sage_attn() drop-in replacement for PyTorch inference
infra_impact.llm: 2x attention speedup directly benefits prefill-bound LLM workloads
infra_impact.agent: Reduced attention latency enables faster long-context reasoning

open_questions: How does SageAttention perform on Hopper where FP8 TCs are faster?
Can Smooth K be extended to training backward pass?
What is the optimal mixed-precision strategy for FP4 on Blackwell?
